gauge.name: Meramec River near Pacific

impacts.statement: Near this height, the Husky Corporation and the Pro Roller Company along Industrial Drive at Denton Road begin flooding.
The BNSF RR tracks act as a levee protecting the eastern two-thirds of Pacific.  Near this height, these tracks may be overtopped.
Near this height, Interstate 44 may close in both directions east of mile marker 254.0.  This occurred during the December 2015 flood.
Backwater from Calvey Creek will force the closure of Missouri State Route O east of Robertsville near this height.
Railroad tracks begin to flood.  This did not happen in 2015 as water
The clubhouse at the St. Louis Skeet and Trap Club begins to flood.
Near this height, the Graphic Packaging International Company begins flooding at Denton Road and Jefferson Street.
The Olympic Shooting Facility at the St. Louis Skeet and Trap Club is flooded.
East Osage Street (Route 66) is closed.
Missouri State Route O west of Robertsville State Park begins flooding near this level.
20 to 30 residences become subject to flooding.  Major flooding begins.
Denton Road from the Industrial Park south is closed.
East Congress between Columbus and Olive are flooded in City of Pacific.
Highway F & O at Brush Creek is flooded and closed.
Water begins flooding the grounds at the St. Louis Skeet and Trap Club east of Pacific.
The lift station property at East Congress and Elm Street begins flooding near this height.
Four houses near the gage begin to flood at this stage.
Jefferson Street extension is flooded and closed.
Horneker Road begins to flood near this height.
The left bank begins to overflow.
Private road under the Highway F bridge begins flooding near this height.
State Highway AK begins flooding near the intersection with State Highway O near this height.
Opeechee Beach Road is flooded and closed.
Opeechee Beach Road begins flooding.
Route N begins to flood.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
